content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 5, 1993

Catherine Barankin
California Coalition for Children's
Safety & Health
400 Capitol Mall, Suite 900
Sacramento, CA 98514

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-368

Dear Ms. Barankin:

You have requested advice concerning the lobbying disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

Does the California Coalition for Children's Safety and
Health (the "Coalition") qualify as a lobbying coalition by hiring
Sacramento Advocacy, a lobbying firm, to perform administrative
work?

CONCLUSION

The Coalition does not qualify as a lobbying coalition;
therefore, it is not required to register.

FACTS

The Coalition, which is an on-going organization consisting
of representatives from insurance companies, children advocacy
groups, and consumer safety organizations, hired Sacramento
Advocacy, a registered lobbying firm and management company, to
perform the administrative work for the Coalition. Each member of
the Coalition employs its own lobbyist or lobbying firm to
represent its own particular lobbying interests.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Catherine Barankin
A-93-368
page 2

ANALYSIS

Regulation 18616 defines a "lobbying coalition" as:

A group of 10 or more persons formed primarily to influence
legislative or administrative action, whose members make
payments to the coalition for the purpose of sharing the
expenses of employing a lobbyist or contracting for the
services of a lobbying firm.

Because the Coalition is not primarily formed to influence
legislative or administrative action, and does not compensate
Sacramento Advocacy to lobby on its behalf, it is not required to
register as a lobbying coalition.

If you have any questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel


by: Colleen McGee
    Political Reform Consultant
    Technical Assistance Division
